summary: sec-e2e-027 is already defined in the intent graph and no intent-side semantic change is required. Delivery acceptance on 2026-06-07 found implementation-architecture GAPs that must be handed back to Implementation Design: one live QwenPaw runtime was projected as multiple Security Center clients, the runtime heartbeat client could be falsely marked DIVERGED with recovery_gate_status OPEN while the terminal was online and no fork occurred, and the latest live acceptance shows Security Center API projecting an expired lease in overview/timeline while the durable store still keeps last_heartbeat_at=0, lease_expires_at=0, and trust_state=ALIGNED. Implementation Design must reopen sec-e2e-027 delivery, preserve the single canonical Security Center terminal identity, and require the recovery-handshake API contract to persist lease_ttl_seconds-derived heartbeat timing so deploy/api can autonomously downgrade the stopped runtime to UNTRUSTED after TTL expiry.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-027-lease-expiry-active-defense
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Start a QwenPaw edge runtime and let the product background Heartbeat Emitter register an initial lease heartbeat without any user prompt or showcase-specific warmup phrase, then stop or miss subsequent heartbeat emission past the configured TTL, reconnect the same client, and attempt to resume model access before completing the required missing-gap verification.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The separate Security Center projection first shows exactly one canonical client for one live QwenPaw runtime, with nonzero last_heartbeat_at and lease_expires_at, recovery_required=false, recovery_gate_status closed, and no DIVERGED or local_hash_mismatch state while the terminal is online and no fork occurred. After the same canonical client misses heartbeat past the lease TTL, Security Center shows that client as UNTRUSTED; reconnect attempts are denied at model-access scope until full missing-gap verification succeeds, and normal model access is restored only after continuity is proven against the cloud-side shadow state.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: Freeze the explicit testcase to tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_lease_expiry_blocks_untrusted_rejoin_until_gap_sync, executed through the real app_server fixture and observed through the separate deploy/api and deploy/web Security Center boundary. The testcase must not require a normal user to send prompt text that mentions lease heartbeat monitoring or lease window expiry merely to register the lease heartbeat.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Implementation Design must preserve the current cloud-edge collaboration boundary and update the architecture contract so src/qwenpaw/security owns a product background heartbeat emitter and one canonical Security Center client identity: startup must send one lease heartbeat automatically, periodic emission must continue while the runtime is alive, and heartbeat, recovery preflight, lockdown projection, restored-access projection, and timeline reads must converge on the same Security Center terminal record. Browser or console session ids may remain audit metadata, but they must not create separate terminal identities. The deploy/api recovery-handshake request contract must preserve lease_ttl_seconds from runtime heartbeat payloads and persist last_heartbeat_at plus lease_expires_at in the durable Security Center store, not merely project lease timing from updated_at_ns in read models. The explicit testcase or supporting contract guard must fail if lease registration depends on classify_lease_prompt warmup wording, if one online runtime appears as multiple Security Center clients, if a live non-forked runtime heartbeat is marked DIVERGED with recovery_gate_status OPEN, or if an expired lease remains ALIGNED because durable lease fields stayed zero. Prompt-triggered warmup may remain only as a compatibility/test helper, not as the product control point.

openQuestions.question: Should lease heartbeat registration be triggered by a normal user's prompt text?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: No. The product control point must be edge runtime startup plus background periodic emission; prompt-triggered warmup may remain only as a non-product compatibility helper if needed.
openQuestions.reason: The intent graph models Heartbeat Emitter as an application component under Audit Uplink Manager, not as a user-visible conversational command. A user who does not know this security feature must still receive active-defense protection.

openQuestions.question: What startup behavior should Implementation Design freeze for sec-e2e-027?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: On edge runtime startup, emit one lease heartbeat automatically using a stable client identity, then continue emitting periodically while the runtime is alive.
openQuestions.reason: The live showcase found an ALIGNED client with trusted anchors but last_heartbeat_at and lease_expires_at still zero. That state proves high-risk actions can establish trust evidence without registering the lease needed for active defense.

openQuestions.question: What API contract must Implementation Design freeze for runtime heartbeat lease registration?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: The deploy/api recovery-handshake request model must accept and preserve lease_ttl_seconds from runtime heartbeat payloads, and deploy/api/store.py must write durable last_heartbeat_at, lease_ttl_seconds, and lease_expires_at fields whenever a heartbeat lease is registered.
openQuestions.reason: Live acceptance showed overview and timeline projecting last_heartbeat_at and lease_expires_at from updated_at_ns while D:\QwenPawDemo\security-center-data\security-center-store.json still persisted last_heartbeat_at=0 and lease_expires_at=0. Because _apply_lease_expiry only downgrades when durable last_heartbeat_at is greater than zero, the stopped runtime remained ALIGNED after the lease was already expired.

openQuestions.question: Should lease expiry, UNTRUSTED state, and gap verification remain owned by the separate Security Center boundary?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: Yes. Keep the decision and observation path in the separate deploy/api and deploy/web Security Center boundary.
openQuestions.reason: The current intent graph models Security Center, Audit Lease Monitor, and Cloud Integrity Stub as a cross-boundary collaboration. Moving that behavior into the edge runtime would redefine both the architecture boundary and the testcase observation point.

openQuestions.question: What reconnect gate should apply after lease expiry?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: Block all model access until the missing-gap verification succeeds.
openQuestions.reason: The intent testcase input and expected result both use resume model access and regain normal model access, which is stronger than only blocking high-risk tools and is the controlling acceptance boundary for sec-e2e-027.

openQuestions.question: Should one live QwenPaw runtime appear as both a runtime heartbeat client and a session client in Security Center?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: No. Implementation Design must require one canonical Security Center terminal identity for one live runtime; browser, console, or authenticated session ids may be retained only as audit metadata.
openQuestions.reason: The intent models Heartbeat Emitter and Audit Uplink Manager as capabilities of the same backend runtime security foundation. Splitting heartbeat and audit preflight across different Security Center clients causes the operator to see two terminals for one runtime and breaks the normal showcase observation point.

openQuestions.question: May a live online runtime heartbeat be classified as DIVERGED merely because its first cloud shadow state was a bootstrap hash?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: No. Bootstrap alignment must converge with the runtime's real audit-chain head without producing DIVERGED, local_hash_mismatch, or recovery_gate_status OPEN unless a real continuity failure, fork, missing gap, or lease expiry has occurred.
openQuestions.reason: During live delivery acceptance, Security Center showed runtime-heartbeat::c37a1999118c4e3c as DIVERGED and OPEN while the runtime was online and no fork occurred. That is a false positive caused by identity and bootstrap-shadow mismatch, not an intent-valid active defense event.

notes: No SystemArchitecture.json edit was required because sec-e2e-027, the Heartbeat Emitter, the Audit Lease Monitor, and the trust-recovery rule are already present in the intent graph.

Delivery-showcase validation found the current product behavior incomplete: a user can perform high-risk operations and establish trusted anchors while Security Center still reports last_heartbeat_at=0 and lease_expires_at=0, so a subsequent offline action may not downgrade through the lease-expiry active-defense path.

Implementation Design must revise the implementation contracts and handoff so the heartbeat emitter is a runtime-owned background capability, not a prompt-classified security scenario hidden behind warmup wording.

The updated testcase control point is runtime startup plus missed background heartbeat. The observation point is automatic lease registration followed by Security Center UNTRUSTED downgrade and recovery gating after TTL expiry.

A second delivery-acceptance GAP was found on 2026-06-07: the live Security Center overview showed client_count=2 for one QwenPaw runtime, with one session-shaped client ALIGNED but last_heartbeat_at=0 and a separate runtime-heartbeat client carrying heartbeat fields while trust_state=DIVERGED, gap_status=DIVERGED, divergence_reason=local_hash_mismatch, and recovery_gate_status=OPEN.

This second GAP contradicts design/KG/ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, which currently claims canonical-runtime-client-id-003 is closed and openGaps is empty. Implementation Design must reopen that item, correct the implementation architecture and handoff, and require behavior-level validation that one live online runtime produces one Security Center terminal and no false DIVERGED fork state.

A third delivery-acceptance GAP was found on 2026-06-07 after heartbeat requests stopped: the 8091 Security Center API returned lease_expires_at older than current time by more than 120 seconds, but trust_state stayed ALIGNED, recovery_required stayed false, and the durable demo store still had last_heartbeat_at=0 and lease_expires_at=0. Implementation Design must treat this as a required API-contract and store-persistence fix, not as a Web refresh issue.

Implementation GAP补齐任务 for Implementation Design: reopen sec-e2e-027 implementation architecture, update deploy/api/ARCHITECTURE.md and design/KG/ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json so deploy/api/app.py RecoveryHandshakeRequest carries lease_ttl_seconds through to deploy/api/store.py, add or revise a behavior-level guard that starts API/Web/runtime, observes durable nonzero lease fields, stops runtime, then observes UNTRUSTED lease_ttl_expired state through overview and timeline, and only then hand off to Coding/Repair.
